title: exp-004-a3: compliant insertion with force feedback
description:
## Hypothesis
Once aligned with the port (from A1 or A2), the insertion phase requires
compliance. Low stiffness + force monitoring should allow the connector
to slide into the port without excessive force. A spiral search pattern
can compensate for small alignment errors.

## Branch
A3 (classical control, force-feedback insertion)

## Changes Planned
- After approach phase, switch to low stiffness
- Monitor wrench for contact detection
- Implement spiral search if initial push fails
- Gradual Z descent with XY compliance

## Expected Outcome
- Tier 3: 50-75 (partial to full insertion)
- No force penalties

title: exp-003-a2: camera-based port detection
description:
## Hypothesis
The 3 wrist cameras (left, center, right) at 1152x1024 provide enough
visual information to detect the target port. If we can estimate port
pose from images, we can servo toward it without ground truth.

## Branch
A2 (classical control, vision-based)

## Changes Planned
- Capture camera images from observations
- Try template matching / feature detection for port location
- Estimate port pose relative to camera frame
- Command robot to align with detected port

## Expected Outcome
- Port detection working -> proximity points
- Depends on A1 results showing approach strategy works

title: methodology: design iterative experiment loop
description:
## Context
We need a formal methodology for the AIC hackathon that turns experimentation
into a systematic loop. The harness should be able to run itself -- trying
approaches, scoring them, logging results, and adjusting.

## Task
1. Determine how local scoring works (aic_engine + Gazebo)
2. Design the experiment loop: propose -> implement -> score -> log -> adjust
3. Write methodology to .claude/refs/methodology.md
4. Codify into a /experiment skill
5. Set up beads as research log (each experiment = bead with results)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] methodology.md written with formal experiment protocol
- [ ] /experiment skill created
- [ ] Beads structure defined for research logging
- [ ] Clear pathway from "idea" to "scored result" to "next idea"

title: exp-005-b1: collect CheatCode demonstrations for ACT training
exp-006-c1: hybrid vision approach + classical insertion
infra: docker setup for headless CPU-only eval
exp-001-a0: get Tier 1 passing with minimal policy
description:
## Hypothesis
A minimal policy that extends aic_model.Policy, implements insert_cable(),
and sends at least one valid MotionUpdate should pass Tier 1 (1 point) and
potentially score proximity points if it moves toward the port at all.

## Branch
A0 (pre-branch baseline)

## Changes Planned
- Create our own policy package directory
- Implement simplest possible Policy subclass
- Send a single Cartesian command to current TCP position
- Verify Tier 1 passes

## Expected Outcome
- Tier 1: 1 (pass)
- Tier 2: 0 (no meaningful motion)
- Tier 3: 0-10 (might get proximity if starting position is close)
- Total: 1-11 per trial, 3-33 total

title: exp-002-a1: move toward port using task TF data
description:
## Hypothesis
During training (ground_truth:=true), TF frames for ports are published.
We can use controller_state.tcp_pose to know where we are, and compute a
direction vector from TCP to port. Moving along that vector should get us
proximity points (0-25) and possibly partial insertion.

NOTE: TF is NOT available during eval. This experiment validates whether
simple approach-toward-port works, before we invest in camera perception.

## Branch
A1 (classical control, ground-truth bootstrap)

## Changes Planned
- Look up port TF frame during insert_cable
- Compute vector from TCP to port
- Send Cartesian position commands stepping toward port
- Log distance over time

## Expected Outcome
- Tier 3: 25-75 (proximity to full insertion)
- Provides upper bound for classical approach
